title: Schedule of Cost of Goods Manufactured
event: Ortiz Manufacturing: raw materials began at $8,000, purchases were $42,000, and raw materials ended at $6,000. Direct labor was $30,000 and factory overhead $26,000. Work in process began at $12,000 and ended at $9,000. Compute cost of goods manufactured.

computationPaths.narration: Three inventories, one river: materials flow into WIP, WIP flows into finished goods. COGM is what EXITS the factory floor.

memorize.body: DM used = beg RM + purchases − end RM. COGM = total mfg costs + beg WIP − end WIP.
Every stage is the same pattern: beginning + additions − ending = what moved on. Learn it once, use it three times.

questions.prompt: Total manufacturing costs $100,000, beginning WIP $12,000, ending WIP $9,000. COGM = ?

distractors.feedback: You ignored the WIP change. Finishing more than you started this period pushes COGM ABOVE current costs: +12,000 − 9,000.
WIP backwards — ADD beginning (finished this period) and SUBTRACT ending (still on the floor).
